     conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO component_versions (component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n            VALUES (?, 1, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n        )\n\n        conn.commit()\n\n        return Component(\n            id=component_id,\n            name=name,\n )\n\n    def get_component(self, component_id: str) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"Get a component by ID.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, last_used, usage_count \"\n            \"FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n            (component_id,)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if row:\n            return Component(\n                id=row[0],\n                name=row[1],\n                description=row[2],\n                html_template=row[3],\n                version=row[4],\n                created_at=row[5],\n                last_used=row[6],\n                usage_count=row[7]\n            )\n        return None\n\n    def find_best_component(\n        self,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: Dict[str, Any],\n        layout_bucket: str = 'normal'\n    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Find the best-fit component for a result.\n\n        Uses semantic matching on component descriptions and\n        historical usage patterns.\n\n        Args:\n            result_type: The type of result (e.g., \"pod-status\")\n            result_data: The structured result data\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use ('compact', 'normal', 'expanded')\n\n        Returns:\n            The best matching Component, or None if no good match\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # First, check usage patterns for this exact result type and layout\n        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT component_id, match_score\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE result_type = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\n            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n            LIMIT 1\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_type, layout_bucket)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if pattern_row and pattern_row[1] >= 0.7:  # 70% confidence threshold\n            component = self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n            if component:\n                return component\n\n        # Fallback: semantic search on component descriptions\n        # (In production, this would use embeddings. For now, use simple keyword match)\n        components = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT id, name, description FROM components WHERE usage_count > 0\"\n        ).fetchall()\n\n        best_component = None\n        best_score = 0.0\n\n        for comp_id, name, description in components:\n            score = self._semantic_score(result_type, name, description)\n            if score > best_score:\n                best_score = score\n                best_component = self.get_component(comp_id)\n\n        # Only return if we have reasonable confidence\n        if best_score >= 0.5:\n            return best_component\n\n        return None\n\n    def select_component_for_result_type(\n        self,\n        result_type: str,\n        match_threshold: float = 0.7,\n        layout_bucket: str = 'normal',\n    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"Deterministic hot-path selection — the dispatch card selector.\n\n        Returns the highest-``match_score`` component mapped to ``result_type``\n        in ``component_usage_patterns`` whose score clears ``match_threshold``,\n        or None. No LLM, no semantic fallback, no generation (plan: The Hot Path\n        / UI-Regen Agent — \"highest match_score in component_usage_patterns for\n        the result_type, no LLM call\"). A None return is the signal that the\n        result must fall to the built-in generic fallback card.\n\n        Distinct from :meth:`find_best_component`, which the *async* UI-regen\n        agent uses to steward the library (it may fall back to semantic search\n        and ultimately generate a new component). The hot path must never do\n        either — it only reads the recorded mappings.\n\n        Args:\n            result_type: The result type to match against\n            match_threshold: Minimum score threshold (default 0.7)\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket (default 'normal')\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT component_id, match_score\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE result_type = ? AND layout_bucket = ? AND match_score >= ?\n            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n            LIMIT 1\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_type, layout_bucket, match_threshold),\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if not pattern_row:\n            return None\n\n        return self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n\n    def _semantic_score(self, result_type: str, comp_name: str, comp_description: str) -> float:\n        \"\"\"\n        Calculate semantic match score.\n\n        Simple keyword-based scoring. In production, use embeddings.\n        \"\"\"\n        result_type_lower = result_type.lower()\n        comp_name_lower = comp_name.lower()\n        comp_desc_lower = comp_description.lower()\n\n        score = 0.0\n\n        # Exact name match\n        if result_type_lower == comp_name_lower:\n            return 1.0\n\n        # Name contains result type\n        if result_type_lower in comp_name_lower:\n            score += 0.7\n\n        # Description contains keywords from result type\n        result_keywords = result_type_lower.replace('-', ' ').split()\n        for keyword in result_keywords:\n            if keyword in comp_desc_lower:\n                score += 0.1\n\n        return min(score, 1.0)\n\n    def update_component(\n        self,\n        component_id: str,\n        html_template: str,\n        change_note: str\n    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Update a component to a new version.\n\n        Invalidates all cached cards using this component.\n\n        Args:\n            component_id: The component to update\n            html_template: The new template\n            change_note: Why this change was made\n\n        Returns:\n            The updated Component, or None if not found\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # Get current version\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT version FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n            (component_id,)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if not row:\n            return None\n\n        new_version = row[0] + 1\n        now = int(time.time())\n\n        # Update component\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            UPDATE components\n            SET html_template = ?, version = ?, last_used = ?\n            WHERE id = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (html_template, new_version, now, component_id)\n        )\n\n        # Add version history\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO component_versions (component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, new_version, html_template, now, change_note)\n        )\n\n        # Invalidate cached cards\n        conn.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM card_cache WHERE component_id = ?\",\n            (component_id,)\n        )\n\n        conn.commit()\n\n        return self.get_component(component_id)\n\n    def cache_card(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        component_id: str,\n        component_version: int,\n        layout_bucket: str,\n        rendered_html: str\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Cache a rendered card.\n\n        Args:\n            result_id: The result this card renders\n            component_id: The component used\n            component_version: The component version\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket\n            rendered_html: The rendered HTML\n        \"\"\"\n        if layout_bucket not in self.LAYOUT_BUCKETS:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid layout bucket: {layout_bucket}\")\n\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT OR REPLACE INTO card_cache\n            (result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, int(time.time()))\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n        # Update component usage stats\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            UPDATE components\n            SET usage_count = usage_count + 1, last_used = ?\n            WHERE id = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (int(time.time()), component_id)\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n    def get_cached_card(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        component_id: str,\n        layout_bucket: str\n    ) -> Optional[CachedCard]:\n        \"\"\"Get a cached card if it exists and is up-to-date.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT result_id, component_id, component_version, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at\n            FROM card_cache\n            WHERE result_id = ? AND component_id = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if row:\n            return CachedCard(\n                result_id=row[0],\n                component_id=row[1],\n                component_version=row[2],\n                layout_bucket=row[3],\n                rendered_html=row[4],\n                created_at=row[5]\n            )\n        return None\n\n    def invalidate_result(self, result_id: str):\n        \"\"\"Invalidate all cached cards for a result.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        conn.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM card_cache WHERE result_id = ?\",\n            (result_id,)\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n    def record_usage_pattern(\n        self,\n        component_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        match_score: float,\n        layout_bucket: str = 'normal',\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Record a component usage pattern for future matching.\n\n        Args:\n            component_id: The component that was used\n            result_type: The type of result it rendered\n            match_score: How well it matched (0-1)\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket used (default 'normal')\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        now = int(time.time())\n\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO component_usage_patterns (result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, match_score, sample_count, updated_at)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 1, ?)\n            ON CONFLICT(result_type, component_id, layout_bucket)\n            DO UPDATE SET\n                match_score = (match_score * sample_count + ?) / (sample_count + 1),\n                sample_count = sample_count + 1,\n                updated_at = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, match_score, now, match_score, now)\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n\n    def list_components(self, limit: int = 50) -> List[Component]:\n        \"\"\"List all components, ordered by usage.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        rows = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT id, name, description, html_template, version, created_at, last_used, usage_count\n            FROM components\n            ORDER BY usage_count DESC, last_used DESC\n            LIMIT ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (limit,)\n        ).fetchall()\n\n        return [\n            Component(\n                id=row[0],\n                name=row[1],\n                description=row[2],\n                html_template=row[3],\n                version=row[4],\n                created_at=row[5],\n                last_used=row[6],\n                usage_count=row[7]\n            )\n            for row in rows\n        ]\n\n    def get_component_history(self, component_id: str) -> List[ComponentVersion]:\n        \"\"\"Get version history for a component.\"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        rows = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note\n            FROM component_versions\n            WHERE component_id = ?\n            ORDER BY version DESC\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id,)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        return [\n            ComponentVersion(\n                component_id=row[0],\n                version=row[1],\n                html_template=row[2],\n                created_at=row[3],\n                change_note=row[4]\n            )\n            for row in rows\n        ]\n\n    def rollback_component(self, component_id: str, to_version: int) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Rollback a component to a previous version.\n\n        Args:\n            component_id: The component to rollback\n            to_version: The version to rollback to\n\n        Returns:\n            The rolled-back Component, or None if not found\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # Get the target version\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT html_template FROM component_versions\n            WHERE component_id = ? AND version = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, to_version)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if not row:\n            return None\n\n        html_template = row[0]\n\n        # Rollback by updating with the old template as a new version\n        return self.update_component(\n            component_id,\n            html_template,\n            f\"Rollback to version {to_version}\"\n        )\n\n    def close(self):\n        \"\"\"Close the database connection.\"\"\"\n        if self._conn:\n            self._conn.close()\n            self._conn = None\n\n\n# Singleton instance for the application\n_library_instance: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None\n\n\ndef get_library(db_path: str = '/home/coding/aide-de-camp/data/components.db') -> ComponentLibrary:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the component library singleton.\"\"\"\n    global _library_instance\n    if _library_instance is None:\n        _library_instance = ComponentLibrary(db_path)\n    return _library_instance\n","numLines":658,"startLine":1,"totalLines":658}}}

All state is persisted\n206\t-- so a watcher restart loses nothing -- breaker state lives in the database,\n207\t-- not only in memory.\n208\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_watch (\n209\t    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- References bead ID (intents.bead_ref)\n210\t    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of REFUSED: comments seen\n211\t    last_refusal_reason TEXT,  -- Most recent refusal reason\n212\t    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,  -- Timestamp of most recent refusal\n213\t    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,  -- Latest comment index processed (-1 = none)\n214\t    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Unix timestamp when SLA expires\n215\t    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when SLA was flagged (NULL if not flagged)\n216\t    fenced_at          INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when bead was fenced to status=blocked (NULL if not fenced)\n217\t    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL  -- When this watch row was created\n218\t);\n219\t\n220\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch(sla_deadline);\n221\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch(fenced_at);\n222\t\n223\t-- Card cache: pre-rendered HTML for result components\n224\t-- Stores server-side rendered HTML for result/component/layout combinations.\n225\t-- Primary key (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket) allows multiple cached\n226\t-- variations per result (e.g., different layouts). Populated after component\n227\t-- selection to avoid repeated rendering for the same result.\n228\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS card_cache (\n229\t    result_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\n230\t    component_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n231\t    layout_bucket  TEXT NOT NULL,\n232\t    rendered_html  TEXT NOT NULL,\n233\t    created_at     INTEGER NOT NULL,\n234\t    PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n235\t);\n236\t\n237\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_card_cache_result_id ON card_cache(result_id);\n238\t\"\"\"\n239\t\n240\t# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n241\t# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\n242\tDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n243\t    \"router_ms\",\n244\t    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n245\t    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n246\t    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n247\t    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n248\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n249\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n250\t    \"stt_ms\",\n251\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n252\t)\n253\t\n254\t# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\n255\tINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n256\t\n257\t# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n258\t# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n259\t# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\n260\tDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n261\t    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n284\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n285\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n286\t\n287\t            # Create schema\n288\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n289\t            await db.commit()\n290\t\n291\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n292\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n293\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n294\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n295\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n296\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n297\t\n298\t    @staticmethod\n299\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n300\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n301\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n302\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n303\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n304\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n305\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n306\t            await db.execute(\n307\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n308\t            )\n309\t\n310\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n311\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n312\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n313\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n314\t\n315\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n316\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n317\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n318\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n319\t\n320\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n336\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n337\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n338\t            status_col = None\n339\t            for col in cols:\n340\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n341\t                    status_col = col\n342\t                    break\n343\t\n344\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n345\t        if not status_col:\n346\t            return\n347\t\n348\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n349\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n350\t        async with db.execute(\n351\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n352\t        ) as cur:\n353\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n354\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n355\t                # Already migrated\n356\t                return\n357\t\n358\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n359\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n360\t\n361\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n362\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n363\t\n364\t        try:\n365\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n366\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n367\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n368\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n369\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n370\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n371\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n372\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n373\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n374\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n375\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n376\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n377\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n378\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n379\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n380\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n381\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n382\t                )\n383\t            \"\"\")\n384\t\n385\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n386\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n387\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n388\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n389\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n390\t                FROM intents\n391\t            \"\"\")\n392\t\n393\t            # Recreate indexes\n394\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n395\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n396\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n397\t\n398\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n399\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n400\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n401\t\n402\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n403\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n404\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n405\t\n406\t            await db.commit()\n407\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n408\t\n409\t        except Exception as e:\n410\t            await db.rollback()\n411\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n412\t            raise\n413\t\n414\t    @staticmethod\n415\t    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n416\t        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n417\t\n418\t        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n419\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n420\t        \"\"\"\n421\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n422\t        async with db.execute(\n423\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n424\t        ) as cur:\n425\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n426\t            if not table_sql:\n427\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n428\t\n429\t            # If intent_id is already nullable (NOT NULL not present), skip migration\n430\t            if \"intent_id   TEXT,\" in table_sql[0] or \"intent_id TEXT,\" in table_sql[0]:\n431\t                if \"NOT NULL\" not in table_sql[0].split(\"intent_id\")[1].split(\",\")[0]:\n432\t                    # Already migrated\n433\t                    return\n434\t\n435\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n436\t        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n437\t\n438\t        # With DEFAULT -1, no comments are skipped\n506\t        initially, and only comments with index > comment_high_water are processed.\n507\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN DEFAULT, so we recreate the table.\n508\t        \"\"\"\n509\t        # Table doesn't exist yet\n516\t\n517\t            # If comment_high_water already has DEFAULT -1, skip migration\n518\t            if \"DEFAULT -1\" in table_sql[0]:\n519\t                # Already migrated\n520\t                return\n521\t\n522\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new default\n523\t        logger.info(\"Migrating bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\")\n524\t\n525\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n526\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n527\t\n528\t        try:\n529\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n545\t            # Existing rows with comment_high_water=0 are migrated as-is\n546\t            # (they represent \"comment 0 has been processed\", which is valid)\n547\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n548\t                INSERT INTO bead_watch_new\n549\t                SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n550\t            \"\"\")\n551\t\n552\t            # NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n1503\t        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n1504\t        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n1505\t        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n1506\t        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n1507\t\n1508\t        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n1509\t        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n1510\t        \"\"\"\n1511\t        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n1512\t\n1513\t        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n1514\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1515\t            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n1516\t                if since is not None:\n1517\t                    query = (\n1518\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1519\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ?
