When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above\nthreshold) the result is *flagged* (``card_fallback``) and the client renders\nthe built-in generic fallback card — a novel shape never blanks the canvas.\n\nScope of writes (plan, Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card +\nUI-Regen Agent): the hot-path renderer is the sole writer of ``card_cache`` and\nthe component usage stats (``components.usage_count`` / ``last_used`` and\n``component_usage_patterns``). It NEVER writes component definitions\n(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\nUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport html\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\nDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n\n# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\nDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n\n# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n\n\ndef derive_result_type(\n    intent_type: Optional[str],\n    project_slug: Optional[str],\n    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n\n    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n\n    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n    \"\"\"\n    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RenderOutcome:\n    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n\n    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n    component_id: Optional[str]\n    card_fallback: bool\n    layout_bucket: str\n\n\nclass HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n\n    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    ):\n        self.library = library or get_library()\n        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n\n    def render(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n\n        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n        component DB.\n        \"\"\"\n        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n\n        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n            result_type, self.match_threshold\n        )\n        if component is None:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n                result_type,\n                self.match_threshold,\n                result_id,\n            )\n            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n                summary=summary,\n                data=result_data,\n                urgency=urgency,\n            )\n            return RenderOutcome(\n                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n                component_id=None,\n                card_fallback=True,\n                layout_bucket=bucket,\n            )\n\n        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n        self.library.cache_card(\n            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n        )\n        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n            result_id,\n            component.id,\n            component.version,\n            result_type,\n        )\n        return RenderOutcome(\n            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n            component_id=component.id,\n            card_fallback=False,\n            layout_bucket=bucket,\n        )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n\n    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not template:\n        return \"\"\n\n    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n        path = match.group(1).strip()\n        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n        if value is None:\n            return \"\"\n        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n\n    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n\n\ndef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n    value: Any = data\n    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n        if isinstance(value, dict):\n            value = value.get(key)\n        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n            value = value[int(key)]\n        else:\n            return None\n    return value\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n    if value is None:\n        return ''\n    if isinstance(value, str):\n        return value\n    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n        return str(value)\n    try:\n        return json.dumps(value)\n    except Exception:\n        return str(value)\n\n\ndef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n    if data is None:\n        return []\n\n    obj = data\n\n    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n    if isinstance(data, str):\n        try:\n            obj = json.loads(data)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            return [('value', data)]\n\n    # Handle array\n    if isinstance(obj, list):\n        rows = []\n        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle object\n    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n        rows = []\n        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle primitive\n    return [('value', str(obj))]\n\n\ndef render_fallback_card(\n    summary: str | None = None,\n    data: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    urgency: str | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Render the generic fallback card HTML (server-side version of createFallbackCard).\n\n    This generates the same HTML structure as the client-side createFallbackCard()\n    function in canvas.js, ensuring the fallback card can be streamed via SSE\n    and injected directly into the canvas without a blank canvas state.\n\n    All dynamic values are HTML-escaped per the escaping contract.\n\n    Args:\n        summary: Optional result summary\n        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n\n    Returns:\n        HTML string for the fallback card\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Build fallback card HTML\n        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n\n        # Header with icon and title\n        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-icon\">{html.escape(\"🗒️\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-title\">{html.escape(\"Result\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Summary paragraph\n        if summary:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary, quote=False)}</p>')\n\n        # Key/value grid from data\n        if data:\n            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n            if rows:\n                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n                for key, value in rows:\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n                card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Urgency badge\n        if urgency:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <span class=\"urgency-badge {html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}\">{html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}</span>')\n\n        card_parts.append('</div>')\n\n        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        # Fallback render failures should not crash the dispatch flow\n        logger.error(f\"Fallback card render failed: {e}\")\n        # Return minimal safe fallback HTML\n        return f'<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\"><p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary or \"Result\", quote=False)}</p></div>'\n\n\ndef select_rendered_card(\n    result_type: str,\n    result_data: dict[str, Any],\n    result_id: str,\n    layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n) -> Optional[str]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic component selector: (result_type, result.data) → Optional[rendered_html].\n\n    Pure SQL lookup — no LLM call in this path. Queries component_usage_patterns\n    for the highest match_score where result_type matches. If found and score above\n    threshold: fills component template with result.data, writes to card_cache, and\n    returns rendered HTML. If no match or below threshold: returns None (triggers\n    fallback rendering by the caller).\n\n    This is the server-side deterministic component selector specified in the task\n    acceptance criteria. It returns Optional[str] directly, unlike render() which\n    returns a RenderOutcome object with additional metadata.\n\n    Args:\n        result_type: The result type to match against component_usage_patterns\n        result_data: The result data dict to fill into the component template\n        result_id: The result ID for card_cache write\n        layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use (default: 'normal')\n        match_threshold: Minimum match score threshold (default: 0.7)\n        library: Optional ComponentLibrary instance (uses singleton if not provided)\n\n    Returns:\n        Rendered HTML string if a component matched, None if no match or below threshold\n    \"\"\"\n    lib = library or get_library()\n\n    # Query component_usage_patterns for highest match_score where result_type matches\n    component = lib.select_component_for_result_type(\n        result_type=result_type,\n        match_threshold=match_threshold,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n    )\n\n    # If no component matched or below threshold, return None (triggers fallback)\n    if component is None:\n        logger.debug(\n            \"select_rendered_card: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n            \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n            result_type,\n            match_threshold,\n            result_id,\n        )\n        return None\n\n    # On match: fill component template with result.data\n    rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n    # Write to card_cache\n    lib.cache_card(\n        result_id=result_id,\n        component_id=component.id,\n        component_version=component.version,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n        rendered_html=rendered_html,\n    )\n\n    # Record usage pattern (hot-path match is high confidence, so score=1.0)\n    lib.record_usage_pattern(\n        component_id=component.id,\n        result_type=result_type,\n        match_score=1.0,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n    )\n\n    logger.info(\n        \"select_rendered_card: rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n        result_id,\n        component.id,\n        component.version,\n        result_type,\n    )\n\n    return rendered_html\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Singleton\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_renderer: Optional[HotPathRenderer] = None\n\n\ndef get_renderer(\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n    reset: bool = False,\n) -> HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the process-wide hot-path renderer singleton.\n\n    Pass ``library=`` (and ``reset=True``) to rebind it — how tests point the\n    renderer at an isolated component DB without touching the production\n    ``data/components.db`` singleton.\n    \"\"\"\n    global _renderer\n    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n    return _renderer\n","numLines":431,"startLine":1,"totalLines":431}}}

Below-threshold case - component found but score < threshold\n500\t    \"\"\"\n501\t\n502\t    def test_match_case_returns_rendered_html(self, component_library, sample_component, sample_result_data):\n503\t        \"\"\"TC-SRC-001: Match case - component found with score >= threshold returns rendered HTML.\n504\t\n505\t        When a component exists in component_usage_patterns with match_score >= threshold,\n506\t        select_rendered_card should:\n507\t        - Fill the component template with result.data\n508\t        - Write to card_cache\n509\t        - Return the rendered HTML string\n510\t        \"\"\"\n511\t        result_id = \"result-test-123\"\n512\t        result_type = \"status:test-project\"\n513\t\n514\t        # Render-Path Security\n311\t# =============================================================================\n312\t\n313\tclass testHTMLEscapingInFallbackCard:\n314\t    \"\"\"Test that HTML escaping works in the fallback card per the escaping contract.\"\"\"\n315\t\n316\t    def test_fallback_card_escapes_html_in_data_values(self):\n317\t        \"\"\"TC-FB-009: Fallback card should escape HTML in result.data values.\"\"\"\n318\t        # Simulate result data with HTML-like strings\n319\t        result_data = {\n320\t            \"log_line\": '<script>alert(\"xss\")</script>',\n321\t            \"error\": \"Error: <div>Some markup</div>\",\n322\t        }\n323\t\n324\t        # The fallback card renderer should:\n325\t        # 1. Iterate over result.data key/value pairs\n326\t        # 2. Escape each value using escapeHtml()\n327\t        # 3. Render escaped values as text nodes (not raw HTML)\n328\t\n329\t        # Verify escaping contract:\n330\t        # - '<' becomes '&lt;'\n331\t        # - '>' becomes '&gt;'\n332\t        # - '\"' becomes '&quot;'\n333\t        # - '&' becomes '&amp;'\n334\t\n335\t        escaped_log = result_data[\"log_line\"].replace('<', '&lt;').replace('>', '&gt;')\n336\t        assert '&lt;script&gt;' in escaped_log\n337\t        assert '<script>' not in escaped_log\n338\t\n339\t    def test_fallback_card_preserves_safe_text(self):\n340\t        \"\"\"TC-FB-010: Fallback card should preserve safe text as-is.\"\"\"\n341\t        result_data = {\n342\t            \"message\": \"All systems operational\",\n343\t            \"count\": 42,\n344\t        }\n345\t\n346\t        # Safe text should be rendered as-is (no escaping needed)\n347\t        assert result_data[\"message\"] == \"All systems operational\"\n348\t        assert result_data[\"count\"] == 42\n349\t\n350\t\n351\t# =============================================================================\n352\t# Integration Tests - Iterate over result.data key/value pairs\n        # 2. Escape each value using escapeHtml()\n        # 3. Render escaped values as text nodes (not raw HTML)\n\n        # Verify escaping contract:\n        # - '<' becomes '&lt;'\n        # - '>' becomes '&gt;'\n        # - '\"' becomes '&quot;'\n        # - '&' becomes '&amp;'\n\n        escaped_log = result_data[\"log_line\"].replace('<', '&lt;').replace('>', '&gt;')\n        assert '&lt;script&gt;' in escaped_log\n        assert '<script>' not in escaped_log\n\n    def test_fallback_card_preserves_safe_text(self):\n        \"\"\"TC-FB-010: Fallback card should preserve safe text as-is.\"\"\"\n        result_data = {\n            \"message\": \"All systems operational\",\n            \"count\": 42,\n        }\n\n        # Safe text should be rendered as-is (no escaping needed)\n        assert result_data[\"message\"] == \"All systems operational\"\n        assert result_data[\"count\"] == 42\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# Integration Tests - error.\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport sys\nimport time\nimport httpx\n\nBASE = \"http://localhost:8000\"\nTIMEOUT = 30  # seconds to wait for result_created\n\n\nasync def run(utterance: str) -> bool:\n    async with httpx.AsyncClient(base_url=BASE, timeout=10) as client:\n        # 1. Register a surface\n        session_id = f\"e2e-test-{int(time.time())}\"\n        reg = await client.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/register\", json={\n            \"session_id\": session_id,\n            \"surface_type\": \"canvas\",\n        })\n        reg.raise_for_status()\n        surface_id = reg.json()[\"surface_id\"]\n        print(f\"  session:  {session_id}\")\n        print(f\"  surface:  {surface_id}\")\n\n    # 2. Open SSE stream and dispatch concurrently\n    result_event = asyncio.Event()\n    received: dict = {}\n\n    async def listen_sse():\n        url = f\"{BASE}/api/v1/sse?surface_id={surface_id}&session_id={session_id}&surface_type=canvas\"\n        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=None) as sse_client:\n            async with sse_client.stream(\"GET\", url) as resp:\n                event_type = None\n                async for line in resp.aiter_lines():\n                    if line.startswith(\"event:\"):\n                        event_type = line.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip()\n                    elif line.startswith(\"data:\") and event_type == \"result_created\":\n                        received.update(json.loads(line.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip()))\n                        result_event.set()\n                        return\n                    elif not line:\n                        event_type = None\n\n    async def dispatch():\n        await asyncio.sleep(0.5)  # Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                router_ms=router_ms,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:","numLines":200,"startLine":100,"totalLines":900}}}

Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n\n            def _on_first_source(_source, _result) -> None:\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_first_source)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # False):\n85\t        await b.start()\n86\t    return b\n87\t\n88\t\n89\tasync def _collect_sse_events(broadcaster, conn, wanted_types, *, timeout=5.0):\n90\t    \"\"\"Collect SSE events until all wanted types arrive.\"\"\"\n91\t    wanted = set(wanted_types)\n92\t    collected = []\n93\t\n94\t    async def drain():\n95\t        async for wire in broadcaster.event_generator(conn):\n96\t            for etype, data in parse_sse_stream(wire):\n97\t                if etype == \"connected\":\n98\t                    continue\n99\t                collected.append((etype, data))\n100\t                if wanted <= {e for e, _ in collected}:\n101\t                    return\n102\t\n103\t    task = asyncio.create_task(drain())\n104\t    try:\n105\t        await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n106\t    except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n107\t        task.cancel()\n108\t        raise\n109\t    return collected\n110\t\n111\t\n112\t# --- Test 1: Known component → cached HTML They ensure:\n\n1. Dispatch with known matching component → cached HTML renders via SSE\n2. Dispatch with novel shape → fallback card renders via SSE\n3. Reset library singleton\n    import src.components.library as lib_mod\n    lib_mod._library_instance = None\n    import src.render.hot_path as render_mod\n    render_mod._renderer = None\n\n    store = store_mod.get_store()\n    await store.initialize()\n\n    # Use the isolated library from the fixture, not the global singleton\n        lib = main_mod._component_library\n        assert lib is not None, \"Component library should be initialized by fixture\"\n\n        # Create a session and surface\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n\n        # Seed a component for status:test-project result type\n        component = lib.create_component(\n            name=\"Status Component\",\n            description=\"Test status component\",\n            html_template='<div class=\"status-card\"><span class=\"status\">{{status}}</span><span class=\"pods\">{{pods}}</span></div>',\n        )\n\n        # Seed usage pattern for result_type \"status:test-project\"\n        lib.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=component.id,\n            result_type=\"status:test-project\",\n            match_score=1.0,  # High confidence match\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\",\n        )\n\n        # Broadcast result_created event (simulating what /dispatch does)\n        b = await _started_broadcaster()\n        conn = b.register(\n            surface_id=surface_id, session_id=session_id, surface_type=\"canvas\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Simulate hot-path render with component match\n            renderer = get_renderer(library=lib)\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=\"result-1\",\n                result_type=\"status:test-project\",\n                result_data={\"status\": \"running\", \"pods\": 3, \"namespace\": \"default\"},\n                summary=\"3 pods running\",\n                urgency=\"normal\",\n            )\n\n            # Should render component HTML (not fallback)\n            assert render_outcome.card_fallback is False\n            # Component ID should match one we created (might not be exact same if multiple)\n            assert render_outcome.component_id is not None\n            assert render_outcome.rendered_html is not None\n            assert \"status-card\" in render_outcome.rendered_html\n            assert \"3\" in render_outcome.rendered_html  # pods value filled in\n            assert \"running\" in render_outcome.rendered_html  # status value filled in\n\n            # Broadcast the event with component rendered HTML\n            await b.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=\"result_created\",\n                    data={\n                        \"intent_id\": \"intent-1\",\n                        \"topic_id\": \"topic-1\",\n                        \"summary\": \"3 pods running\",\n                        \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n                        \"component_id\": component.id,\n                        \"card_fallback\": False,\n                    },\n                    rendered_html=render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                    target_surface_id=surface_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            # Collect SSE events\n            events = await _collect_sse_events(b, conn, [\"result_created\"])\n            assert len(events) >= 1\n\n            etype, data = events[0]\n            assert etype == \"result_created\"\n            assert \"rendered_html\" in data\n            assert data[\"card_fallback\"] is False\n            assert \"status-card\" in data[\"rendered_html\"]\n            assert \"3\" in data[\"rendered_html\"]\n\n            # Verify canvas can render this card\n            card_data = {\n                \"topic\": {\"id\": \"topic-1\", \"label\": \"Pod Status\", \"type\": \"project\"},\n                \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": 5},\n                \"latest_result\": {\n                    \"summary\": \"3 pods running\",\n                    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n                    \"rendered_html\": data[\"rendered_html\"],\n                    \"component_id\": component.id,\n                    \"card_fallback\": False,\n                },\n            }\n\n            rendered = render_cards([card_data])\n            assert len(rendered) == 1\n            html = rendered[0][\"outerHTML\"]\n            assert \"component-card\" in html\n            assert \"status-card\" in html\n            assert \"3\" in html\n            # No blank canvas\n            assert len(html.strip()) > 0\n\n        finally:\n            b.unregister(conn.connection_id)\n\n\n# --- Test 2: Novel shape → fallback card renders -------------------------------\n\n\nclass TestNovelShapeFallback:\n    \"\"\"Test dispatch with novel shape (no component) renders fallback card.\"\"\"\n\n    async def test_novel_shape_renders_fallback_via_dispatch(\n        self, isolated_store\n    ):\n        \"\"\"When result type has no component match, fallback card renders via SSE.\"\"\"\n        store = isolated_store\n        # Use the isolated library from the fixture, not the global singleton\n        lib = main_mod._component_library\n        assert lib is not None, \"Component library should be initialized by fixture\"\n\n        # pods value\n\n        # Second card: fallback\n        assert \"fallback-card\" in rendered[1][\"className\"]\n        assert \"findings\" in htmls[1]\n        assert \"something new\" in htmls[1]\n\n        # Seed usage pattern BEFORE rendering (simulating that this component was already matched)\n        lib.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=component.id,\n            result_type=\"test:write-scope\",\n            match_score=1.0,\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\",\n        )\n\n        # Render with hot path (simulates dispatch)\n        renderer = get_renderer(library=lib)\n        render_outcome = renderer.render(\n            result_id=\"result-scope-test\",\n            result_type=\"test:write-scope\",\n            result_data={\"value\": \"test\"},\n            summary=\"Test summary\",\n            urgency=\"normal\",\n        )\n\n        # Verify hot-path wrote to card_cache\n        # (card_cache table should have an entry)\n        conn = lib._get_conn()\n        result_id = \"result-scope-test\"  # Use the result_id we passed to render()\n        card_cache_rows = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT * FROM card_cache WHERE result_id = ?\",\n            (result_id,)\n        ).fetchall()\n        # Card cache should have entry if component matched\n        if not render_outcome.card_fallback:\n            assert len(card_cache_rows) > 0, \"card_cache should have entry for component match\"\n\n        # Verify hot-path wrote to component_usage_patterns\n        pattern_rows = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT * FROM component_usage_patterns WHERE result_type = ?\",\n            (\"test:write-scope\",)\n        ).fetchall()\n        assert len(pattern_rows) > 0, \"component_usage_patterns should have entry\"\n\n        # Verify hot-path did NOT create new component definitions\n        # (components table should only have our manually created one)\n        final_components = lib.list_components(limit=100)\n        # Count should be initial + 1 (our manually created component)\n        # Hot-path should not have created any additional components\n        assert len(final_components) == initial_component_count + 1\n\n        # Verify usage stats were updated\n        updated_component = lib.get_component(component.id)\n        assert updated_component.usage_count > 0\n        assert updated_component.last_used is not None\n\n\n# --- Test 5: Threshold-based fallback -----------------------------------------\n\n\nclass TestMatchThresholdFallback:\n    \"\"\"Test that components below match_threshold trigger fallback.\"\"\"\n\n    async def test_below_threshold_fallback(\n        self, isolated_store\n    ):\n        \"\"\"Component with match_score below threshold triggers fallback.\"\"\"\n        store = isolated_store\n        # Below 0.7 threshold\n        )\n\n        # Render with default threshold (0.7)\n        renderer = get_renderer(library=lib)\n        render_outcome = renderer.render(\n            result_id=\"result-threshold\",\n            result_type=\"test:low-match\",\n            result_data={\"value\": \"test\"},\n            summary=\"Test threshold\",\n            urgency=\"normal\",\n        )\n\n        # and usage stats, never\nto component definitions (components, component_versions, component_tags tables).\n\nThis separation ensures the hot-path selector and UI-regen agent never race on a\ncomponent's identity — the server only reads component definitions; only the\nUI-regen agent writes them.\n\nWrite scope (plan, Security Model):\n- Server hot-path: card_cache rows + usage stats last_matched, match_score columns\n\n    It must NEVER write:\n    - components rows (except usage_count/last_used columns)\n    - component_versions rows\n    - component_tags rows\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def test_hot_path_never_writes_component_definitions(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test that hot-path render only touches card_cache and usage stats.\n\n        This is the core acceptance test for adc-2jvu write-scope separation.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Create isolated test database\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n            db_path = Path(tmpdir) / html_template=\"<div>Status: {{summary}}</div>\",\n                change_note=\"Initial version for test\"\n            )\n\n            # Seed usage pattern so the hot-path selector will match\n            library.record_usage_pattern(\n                component_id=component.id,\n                result_type=\"status:test-project\",\n                match_score=0.9\n            )\n\n            # Snapshot the database state before render\n            conn = sqlite3.connect(str(db_path))\n            conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\n\n            before_components = conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM components\"\n            ).fetchall()\n            before_versions = conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM component_versions\"\n            ).fetchall()\n     ).fetchall()\n\n            conn.close()\n\n            # Run hot-path render\n            renderer = get_renderer(library=library, reset=True)\n            result_id = \"test-result-123\"\n            result_data = {\n                \"summary\": \"Test status result\",\n                \"details\": \"Some details\"\n            }\n\n            outcome = renderer.render(\n layout_bucket=\"normal\"\n            )\n\n            # Verify render succeeded (not a fallback)\n            assert outcome.component_id == component.id\n            assert outcome.rendered_html is not None\n            assert not outcome.card_fallback\n\n            # Verify write-scope: only card_cache and usage stats changed\n            conn = sqlite3.connect(str(db_path))\n            conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\n\n            after_components = conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM components\"\n            ).fetchall()\n            after_versions = conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM component_versions\"\n            ).fetchall()\n            after_tags = conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM component_tags\"\n            ).fetchall()\n            after_cache = conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM card_cache\"\n            ).fetchall()\n\n            # 1. component_versions: MUST NOT CHANGE (server never writes versions)\n            assert len(after_versions) == len(before_versions) == 1, \\\n                \"hot-path render must not write component_versions\"\n            assert after_versions[0][\"component_id\"] == before_versions[0][\"component_id\"]\n            assert after_versions[0][\"version\"] == before_versions[0][\"version\"]\n            assert after_versions[0][\"html_template\"] == before_versions[0][\"html_template\"]\n\n            # 2. component_tags: MUST NOT CHANGE (server never writes tags)\n            assert len(after_tags) == len(before_tags) == 0, \\\n                \"hot-path render must not write component_tags\"\n\n            # 3. components: only usage_count and last_used may change\n            assert len(after_components) == len(before_components) == 1, \\\n                \"hot-path render must not create new component rows\"\n\n            before_comp = before_components[0]\n            after_comp = after_components[0]\n\n            # These fields MUST NOT change (write-scope boundary)\n            assert after_comp[\"id\"] == before_comp[\"id\"]\n            assert after_comp[\"name\"] == before_comp[\"name\"]\n            assert before_comp[\"version\"]\n            assert after_comp[\"created_at\"] == before_comp[\"created_at\"]\n\n            # These fields MAY change (usage stats are in write scope)\n            assert Server-side fallback HTML\n            assert \"fallback-card\" in outcome.rendered_html  # Verify it's the fallback template\n            assert outcome.card_fallback is True\n\n            # Verify nothing was written\n            conn = sqlite3.connect(str(db_path))\n            after_cache_count = conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM card_cache\"\n            ).fetchone()[0]\n            after_usage_count = conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT usage_count FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n                (component.id,)\n            ).fetchone()[0]\n            conn.close()\n\n            assert after_cache_count == before_cache_count, \\\n                \"fallback must not write card_cache\"\n            assert after_usage_count == before_usage_count, \\\n                \"fallback must not increment usage_count\"\n\n    def test_layout_bucket_uniqueness_in_card_cache(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test that card_cache PK uniqueness holds: (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket).\n\n        Rendering the same result with the same component but different layout buckets\n        should create distinct rows, not overwrite.\n        \"\"\"\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n            db_path = Path(tmpdir) / \"test_components.db\"\n            library = ComponentLibrary(str(db_path))\n\n            component = library.create_component(\n                name=\"multi-layout\",\n                description=\"Multi-layout component\",\n                html_template=\"<div>{{summary}}</div>\",\n            )\n            library.record_usage_pattern(\n                component_id=component.id,\n                result_type=\"status:layout-test\",\n                match_score=0.95\n            )\n\n            renderer = get_renderer(library=library, reset=True)\n            result_id = \"layout-test-result\"\n\n            # component_tags tables).\n\nThis separation ensures the hot-path selector and UI-regen agent never race on a\ncomponent's identity — the server only reads component definitions; only the\nUI-regen rows\n    2. components.usage_count and last_used columns\n    3. component_usage_patterns.sample_count, last_matched, match_score columns\n\n    It must NEVER write:\n    - components rows (except usage_count/last_used columns)\n    - component_versions rows\n    - component_tags rows\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def test_hot_path_never_writes_component_definitions(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test that hot-path render only touches card_cache and usage stats.\n\n        This is the core acceptance test for adc-2jvu write-scope separation.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Create isolated test database\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n            db_path = Path(tmpdir) / \"test_components.db\"\n\n            # Initialize component library\n            library = name=\"test-status\",\n                description=\"Renders status results\",\n                html_template=\"<div>Status: {{summary}}</div>\",\n                change_note=\"Initial version for test\"\n            )\n\n            # Seed usage pattern so the hot-path selector will match\n            library.record_usage_pattern(\n                component_id=component.id,\n                result_type=\"status:test-project\",\n                match_score=0.9\n            )\n\n            # Snapshot the database state before render\n            conn = sqlite3.connect(str(db_path))\n            conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\n\n            before_components = conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM components\"\n            ).fetchall()\n            before_versions = ).fetchall()\n            before_cache = conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM card_cache\"\n            ).fetchall()\n\n            conn.close()\n\n            # Run hot-path render\n
